entry.title: Spectral-Risk Safe Reinforcement Learning with Convergence Guarantees

link.title: Spectral-Risk Safe Reinforcement Learning with Convergence Guarantees

entry.content: We propose a safe RL algorithm with spectral risk constraints, which shows convergence to an optimum in tabular settings.
entry.summary: We propose a safe RL algorithm with spectral risk constraints, which shows convergence to an optimum in tabular settings.

entry.title: D2NAS: Efficient Neural Architecture Search with Performance Improvement and Model Size Reduction for Diverse Tasks

link.title: D2NAS: Efficient Neural Architecture Search with Performance Improvement and Model Size Reduction for Diverse Tasks

entry.content: We introduce D2NAS, Differential and Diverse NAS, leveraging techniques such as Differentiable ARchiTecture Search (DARTS) and Diverse-task Architecture SearcH (DASH) for architecture discovery.
entry.summary: We introduce D2NAS, Differential and Diverse NAS, leveraging techniques such as Differentiable ARchiTecture Search (DARTS) and Diverse-task Architecture SearcH (DASH) for architecture discovery.

entry.title: Tractable and Provably Efficient Distributional Reinforcement Learning with General Value Function Approximation

link.title: Tractable and Provably Efficient Distributional Reinforcement Learning with General Value Function Approximation

entry.content: Our theoretical results show that approximating the infinite-dimensional return distribution with a finite number of moment functionals is the only method to learn the statistical information unbiasedly including nonlinear statistical functional. Second, we propose a provably efficient algorithm, SF-LSVI, achieving a regret bound of $$\tilde{O}(d_E H^{\frac{3}{2}}\sqrt{K})$$.
entry.summary: Our theoretical results show that approximating the infinite-dimensional return distribution with a finite number of moment functionals is the only method to learn the statistical information unbiasedly including nonlinear statistical functional. Second, we propose a provably efficient algorithm, SF-LSVI, achieving a regret bound of \(\tilde{O}(d_E H^{\frac{3}{2}}\sqrt{K})\).

entry.title: Pitfall of Optimism: Distributional Reinforcement Learning by Randomizing Risk Criterion

link.title: Pitfall of Optimism: Distributional Reinforcement Learning by Randomizing Risk Criterion

entry.content: We provide a perturbed distributional Bellman optimality operator by distorting the risk measure and prove the convergence and optimality of the proposed method with the weaker contraction property.
entry.summary: We provide a perturbed distributional Bellman optimality operator by distorting the risk measure and prove the convergence and optimality of the proposed method with the weaker contraction property.

entry.title: SPQR: Controlling Q-ensemble Independence with Spiked Random Model for Reinforcement Learning

link.title: SPQR: Controlling Q-ensemble Independence with Spiked Random Model for Reinforcement Learning

entry.content: By introducing a novel regularization loss for Q-ensemble independence based on random matrix theory, we propose spiked Wishart Q-ensemble independence regularization (SPQR) for reinforcement learning.
entry.summary: By introducing a novel regularization loss for Q-ensemble independence based on random matrix theory, we propose spiked Wishart Q-ensemble independence regularization (SPQR) for reinforcement learning.

entry.title: On the Convergence of Continual Learning with Adaptive Methods

link.title: On the Convergence of Continual Learning with Adaptive Methods

entry.content: In this paper, we provide a convergence analysis of memory-based continual learning with stochastic gradient descent and empirical evidence that training current tasks causes the cumulative degradation of previous tasks.
entry.summary: In this paper, we provide a convergence analysis of memory-based continual learning with stochastic gradient descent and empirical evidence that training current tasks causes the cumulative degradation of previous tasks.

entry.title: Perturbed Quantile Regression for Distributional Reinforcement Learning

link.title: Perturbed Quantile Regression for Distributional Reinforcement Learning

entry.content: We provide a perturbed distributional Bellman optimality operator by distorting the risk measure in action selection, and prove the convergence and optimality of the proposed method by using the weaker contraction property.
entry.summary: We provide a perturbed distributional Bellman optimality operator by distorting the risk measure in action selection, and prove the convergence and optimality of the proposed method by using the weaker contraction property.

entry.title: Adaptive Methods for Nonconvex Continual Learning

link.title: Adaptive Methods for Nonconvex Continual Learning

entry.content: We propose an adaptive method for nonconvex continual learning (NCCL), which adjusts step sizes of both previous and current tasks with the gradients.
entry.summary: We propose an adaptive method for nonconvex continual learning (NCCL), which adjusts step sizes of both previous and current tasks with the gradients.

entry.title: Learning to Learn Unlearned Feature for Brain Tumor Segmentation

link.title: Learning to Learn Unlearned Feature for Brain Tumor Segmentation

entry.content: One of the difficulties in medical image segmentation is the lack of datasets with proper annotations. To alleviate this problem, we propose active meta-tune to achieve balanced parameters for both glioma and brain metastasis domains within a few steps.
entry.summary: One of the difficulties in medical image segmentation is the lack of datasets with proper annotations. To alleviate this problem, we propose active meta-tune to achieve balanced parameters for both glioma and brain metastasis domains within a few steps.

entry.title: Generative Adversarial Trainer: Defense to Adversarial Perturbations with GAN

link.title: Generative Adversarial Trainer: Defense to Adversarial Perturbations with GAN

entry.content: We propose a novel technique to make neural network robust to adversarial examples using a generative adversarial network. We alternately train both classifier and generator networks. The generator network generates an adversarial perturbation that can easily fool the classifier network by using a gradient of each image.
entry.summary: We propose a novel technique to make neural network robust to adversarial examples using a generative adversarial network. We alternately train both classifier and generator networks. The generator network generates an adversarial perturbation that can easily fool the classifier network by using a gradient of each image.
